questions.questionText: Which of the following has the maximum degree of ionisation?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 25�C, the degree of ionisation (dissociation) of pure water is measured as $1.8 \times 10^{-9}$. The value of the dissociation constant, $K_d$, of water at 25�C is

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The values of dissociation constants ($K_a$) for formic acid and acetic acid are $1.77 \times 10^{-4}$ and $1.75 \times 10^{-5}$, respectively. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: At the same concentration, formic acid is 3.18 times stronger than acetic acid.
At the same concentration, acetic acid is 3.18 times stronger than formic acid.
At the same concentration, formic acid is 10.11 times stronger than acetic acid.
At different concentrations, formic acid is 10.11 times stronger than acetic acid.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 298 K, the dissociation constant (Ka) of acetic acid is 0.000018, while that of cyanoacetic acid is 0.0036. If two separate solutions are prepared such that each contains the same number of moles of the respective acid, what should be the ratio of their volumes so that both solutions are isohydric?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
When hydrogen chloride is added to acetic acid, the following equilibrium is formed:

$\mathrm{HCl} + \mathrm{CH_3COOH} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{Cl^-} + \mathrm{CH_3COOH_2^+}$

The correct set representing the conjugate acid–base pairs is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In a mixture of two weak monobasic acids HX (degree of dissociation $\alpha_1$) and HY (degree of dissociation $\alpha_2$) with concentration $C_1$ and $C_2$ and dissociation constants $K_1$ and $K_2$, select correct statement(s).

questions.questionText: Isohydric solutions are those solutions in which the concentration of hydronium ions is equal. If a 0.2 M solution of HA is isohydric with a $4 \times 10^{-4}$ M solution of HCl, then the value of $K_b$ for $A^-$ is

questions.questionText:
How many of the following are amphiprotic?

(i) $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_2^{-}$

(ii) $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_3^{-}$

(iii) $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{PO}_4^{-}$

(iv) $\mathrm{HPO}_3^{2-}$

(v) $\mathrm{HPO}_4^{2-}$

(vi) $\mathrm{NH}_4^{+}$

(vii) $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}_2^{+}$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A mixture of weak acid is 0.1 M in $\mathrm{HCOOH}\left(K_a=1.8 \times\right. \left.10^{-4}\right)$ and 0.1 M in $\mathrm{HOCN}\left(K_a=3.1 \times 10^{-4}\right)$. Hence, $\left(\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{O}^{+}\right)$ is $x \times 10^{-3}$. Thus $x$ is………..
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A weak acid shows $\%$ ionization equal to $1 \%$ in a 1 M solution. If the $\%$ ionization becomes x in a 4 M solution, then find 10x.

questions.questionText: At $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, the dissociation constant of a base BOH is $1.0 \times 10^{-12}$. The concentration of Hydroxyl ions in 0.01 M aqueous solution of the base would be $1.0 \times 10^{-x} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~L}^{-1}$, then $x$ value is :

questions.questionText: For $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~A}, \mathrm{~K}_{\mathrm{a}}=10^{-20}$. The concentration of $\mathrm{A}^{2-}$ in a solution that is 0.01 M in $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~A}$ and 0.1 M in HCl will be $10^{-x} M$, then $x$ value is :
